name: Nexus Ultra
design_notes: This rulebook is optimized for retrieval (RAG): each rule is atomic, tagged, and written as condition->interpretation->action.
Rules are probabilistic: presence of an SL cluster increases probability, but timing/path can be uncertain.
Execution uses hard risk controls: limited attempts, no averaging, and immediate exit on 'mismatch days'.
Bias is a hypothesis; market confirmation has veto power and can force dynamic plan switching.
Plan->Reaction->Adaptation is core: if live behavior mismatches the plan, wait, rebuild structure, then take the second opportunity only after confirmation.
Operator-centric layer: track likely operator entry, holding, and exit behavior; exits often matter more than entries.
SL-hunting is conditional: apply validity filters (retail participation, fresh SL availability, profit distribution, and event risk).
Zone engine reduces overtrading: avoid fair-price conditions; prefer discount/inflated extremes with liquidity confluence.
Thinking engine reduces narrative bias: classify move source (SL hit vs exit vs fresh entry vs operator push) before acting.
Flow engine links moves: current move -> trader reaction -> next move; use it to anticipate where new liquidity forms.
Probability engine selects the highest-probability side using SL availability + validity + zone + confirmation; not the obvious direction.
Planning engine forces scenario thinking (gap up/down/flat + 2-3 day expectation) and prevents rigid bias.
Fake confirmations are expected cost: keep SL strict and let expectancy come from small losses and larger winners (DT-SL-188).

changelog.summary: Added execution adaptability (DT-SL-071..081), operator layer (DT-SL-082..093), and SL-hunting validity filters (DT-SL-094..102).
Added Comfort Engine (DT-SL-103..110), Exit + Next-day Bias Engine (DT-SL-111..120), and Chart Execution Engine (DT-SL-121..131).
Added Thinking Engine (DT-SL-132..139), Zone Engine (DT-SL-140..146), and Adaptive Engine (DT-SL-147..156).
Added Flow Engine (DT-SL-157..167), Probability Engine (DT-SL-168..177), and Planning Engine (DT-SL-178..187).
Added psychology/behavior stack rules (DT-SL-051..070), RAG Loop v20 (LOOP-RAG-005), and fake-confirmation-as-cost discipline (DT-SL-188).
Added round-number wall trap logic, stronger reclaim/acceptance thresholds, deep-gap regime handling, and optional OI wall integration (DT-SL-189..193).
Added micro-sweep/precision stop-hunt handling and stop placement rules to reduce '0-2 point' stop-outs around obvious levels (DT-SL-194..196).

Added option-chain decoding rules: contract-vs-position clarity, strike selection by premium/volume, change-in-OI interpretation, weekly-vs-monthly alignment, and writer 'premium cushion' pain-threshold logic (DT-SL-197..202, MATRIX-OI-001).
Added option-chain fallback-to-chart rules, writer pressure vs comfort (premium expansion), weekly-expiry anchor inference, and sub-round 200/800 level behavior (DT-SL-203..206, MATRIX-OI-002).
Added trap taxonomy matrix (MATRIX-TRAP-001) and integration notes for trap handling; rules implemented in v2.11.0 (DT-SL-207..212).

Implemented trap taxonomy rules and regime-flip logic from transcripts: sideways/whipsaw traps, post-break failure traps, fear/greed filter, and multi-day regime flip (DT-SL-207..212). Expanded trap matrix with news-event trap.
Added fixed risk-reward setup templates for 75-point targets with 30-point SL (DT-SL-213..216) and integrated them into the RAG schema.

Added volatility-era execution upgrades: mandatory profit booking/locking for regular size, closing-price pivot usage, and profit->loss psychology trap handling to reduce givebacks and pattern-overfit (DT-SL-217..221).
Added swing-liquidity mapping rules (swing highs/lows as SL pools), plus 1m execution with 5m confirmation gates and swing reclaim/acceptance triggers (DT-SL-222..225).

rows.meaning: Bearish confirmation; smart-money selling likely active.
rows.action: Prioritize SHORT setups; still require structure confirmation before entry.

rows.meaning: Trap/volatile; direction unclear.
rows.action: Avoid trading OR wait for structure clarity.

rows.meaning: Bullish continuation; smart-money buying likely active.
rows.action: Prioritize LONG setups; still require structure confirmation before entry.

rows.meaning: Trap/volatile; direction unclear.
rows.action: Avoid trading OR wait for structure clarity.

rows.meaning: Cheap/extreme fear; accumulation and reversal probability increases if liquidity confirms.
rows.action: Prefer long ideas after confirmation; allow more aggressive sizing relative to inflated/fair (still within risk limits).

rows.meaning: Balanced price; edge is low; chop and fake moves increase.
rows.action: Avoid trading by default. Trade only with exceptional confirmation + strong confluence.

rows.meaning: Expensive/extreme greed; distribution, traps, or continuation possible depending on operator holding.
rows.action: Trade with caution: either continuation with momentum/holding signatures OR reversal after comfort+trap confirmation.

rows.meaning: Move is driven by stop-loss liquidation; continuation can accelerate via cascade, but reversals can appear after the target-side liquidity is consumed.
rows.action: Trade only after confirmation. If fading, do it only after clear exhaustion + reclaim; otherwise trade with the cascade and capture partials.

rows.meaning: Move is driven by exits (profit booking or forced exit) rather than fresh entries; follow-through can be weaker or mean-reverting.
rows.action: Prefer quick capture and tighter management. Do not assume a new trend until structure confirms.

rows.meaning: Move is driven by new participation; it can become trend if acceptance + holding signatures persist.
rows.action: Trade continuation only after acceptance; avoid late chase. If comfort becomes extreme, prepare for trap logic.

rows.meaning: Move is driven by dominant participant pushing; can be sudden or gradual-build-then-expansion.
rows.action: Align with operator after confirmation; manage exits around operator-exit signatures. Do not fight the push without trap evidence.

rows.meaning: Call writers building while put writers are not building: downside pressure / overhead resistance likely stronger.
rows.action: Treat upper strikes as stronger resistance; prefer sell-on-rise and trap logic unless price shows strong acceptance (DT-SL-190).

rows.meaning: Put writers building while call writers unwind: bullish bias possible.
rows.action: Treat lower strikes as stronger support; prefer long ideas after confirmation. Still defer to chart/structure (DT-SL-028).

rows.meaning: Both sides building: range/mean-reversion regime and trap risk increases.
rows.action: Treat as boundary range (OI walls) and prefer sweep+reclaim / failed acceptance over trend chasing (DT-SL-193/055).

rows.meaning: Both sides unwinding: transition/uncertainty; old OI levels may weaken.
rows.action: Reduce reliance on OI levels; rebuild from price structure + liquidity map (DT-SL-071/147).

rows.meaning: Option premium is low relative to recent reference/expected writer entry; writers are not threatened by small underlying moves.
rows.action: Do not assume OI strike will hold immediately. Expect fake breaks/sweeps around the strike; require sweep+reclaim or strong acceptance (DT-SL-202/190).

rows.meaning: Option premium is expanding rapidly; writer discomfort increases and defense/hedging can change price behavior.
rows.action: Treat the strike as more active: reaction speed increases. Still confirm with chart; do not fade a pressured breakout without trap evidence.

rows.meaning: Low momentum / range hold; both sides wait for big break; the market can farm time + fees and punish impatience.
rows.action: Avoid trading inside the middle; trade only range edges after confirmation or wait for strong acceptance away from the range.

rows.meaning: Fast up-down moves with no clean one-directional follow-through; both sides get chopped.
rows.action: Reduce size/frequency. Avoid adding. Prefer WAIT until structure/zone becomes clearer.

rows.meaning: A breakout triggers crowd buying, then fails and reverses; breakout buyers become liquidity.
rows.action: Do not chase breakout. Prefer short only after failed acceptance + confirmation.

rows.meaning: A breakdown triggers crowd shorting, then fails and reverses; breakdown sellers become liquidity.
rows.action: Do not chase breakdown. Prefer long only after sweep + reclaim + acceptance.

rows.meaning: The trap is primarily in trader mindset (fear of buying high, greed chasing break).
rows.action: Trade only after confirmation + zone alignment; avoid impulse contrarian trades without evidence.

rows.meaning: A trap driven by scheduled news/results/announcements; price can spike both ways and invalidate normal structure/liquidity assumptions.
rows.action: Prefer NO TRADE around the event window. If trading, reduce size, avoid chasing the first impulse, and wait for post-event structure + acceptance before any entry.

loops.name: RAG Loop v18 (Planning + Flow + Probability)
steps: Analyze previous session last 15-30 minutes (momentum, rejections, indecision).
Define an initial hypothesis from Dow structure + key levels (PDH/PDL/round numbers), not a rigid bias.
Create 3 scenarios (gap up / gap down / flat) and a 2-3 day expectation (multi-day thinking).
Apply time intelligence: expiry/holiday/event day risk; reduce size or avoid if event-driven.
Classify current context zone: discount vs fair vs inflated (MATRIX-ZONE-001). Avoid fair zone by default.
Detect likely operator entry zones (sharp turning points) and which side appears dominant.
Map psychology: trapped buyers/sellers, hesitation near key levels, fear-based participation, and crowd/trap risk.
Classify the likely move driver (WHY) using MATRIX-WHY-001: SL hit vs profit booking/exit vs fresh entry vs operator push.
Link flow: for each major move, predict trader reaction and where new liquidity/comfort will form next.
Detect comfort zones/clusters: multi-confirmation alignment (index + constituents + correlated indices).
Build dual scenarios (buyer case vs seller case) and identify likely next liquidity target (which side remains).
Run SL-hunting validity filter: fresh retail participation, SL availability, profit distribution, and no strong event override.
Map liquidity pools: (a) existing SL clusters, (b) newly created SL after breaks, (c) profit-holder liquidity.
Decide SL readiness: immediate vs delayed (1-2 sessions possible) and path type (clean vs trap-heavy).
Run opening filter: gap type + first candle matrix; if trap/volatile, wait or avoid.
If opening behavior is unclear or plan mismatches reality, wait 10-20 minutes and rebuild structure before attempting a second opportunity.
Align timeframes: use higher TF (5/15m) for hypothesis/confirmation and 1m for execution + microstructure validation.
Confirm with multi-index alignment (e.g., NIFTY + BANKNIFTY); reduce conviction on divergence.
Select the highest-probability side using SL map + validity + zone + confirmation (not the obvious side).
Execute only after market confirmation; prefer partial entry scaling; max 1-2 attempts per idea; never average losers; avoid chasing late candles.
Manage trades: take profits when available; watch operator exit vs continuation signatures; if mismatch persists, stop trading for the day.
Before major news/events, avoid holding positions and reduce trading aggressiveness.
Discipline: capture the logical portion (not full move) and cap to 2-3 trades/day unless exceptional clarity exists.

loops.name: RAG Loop v20 (Operator Intent + Comfort + Validity + Exit)
steps: Analyze last session (last 15-30m): momentum, rejections, close quality.
Identify big money/operator direction hypothesis (not certainty).
Detect operator entry/turning points and mark pivots (DT-SL-066).
Map psychology: buyers vs sellers, trapped/risk holders (DT-SL-065).
Map liquidity layers: old SL, new SL after breaks, profit-holder future liquidity (DT-SL-044/045).
Classify likely move driver (WHY) using MATRIX-WHY-001 and anchor it to evidence (DT-SL-133..137).
Run SL-hunting validity: retail participation + fresh SL availability + profit distribution + no event override (DT-SL-094..102).
Detect comfort clusters (multi-confirmation alignment) and wait for trap evidence before contrarian (DT-SL-103..110).
Build dual scenario (buyer case + seller case) and select the next likely target side (DT-SL-113/116).
Predict direction as probability (timing/path uncertain): allow delay + trap-first paths (DT-SL-035/036/038).
Opening validation: gap type + first candle matrix; if trap/volatile, wait/rebuild (MATRIX-OPEN-001).
Confirm behavior: momentum type (SL cascade vs event/flow) and multi-index alignment (DT-SL-043/099/120).
Execute with operator/flow only after price confirmation; expect occasional fake confirmations and treat SL as cost; max 1-2 attempts per idea; no averaging (DT-SL-073/188/039/041).
Manage actively: first capture near ~1R; hold runner only if holding evidence persists (DT-SL-067/088).
Exit fast on operator exit/mismatch signals; stop trading on mismatch day (DT-SL-086/093/049).

frameworks.name: Integrated Master Framework (Dow + SL Hunting + Operator + Psychology)
principles: Plan is a hypothesis; market confirmation has veto power; adapt quickly on mismatch (DT-SL-147/148/071).
SL clusters indicate probability, not timing; path can be trap-first and time can delay 1-2 sessions (DT-SL-035/036/038).
Fake confirmations are expected cost; strict SL + attempt limits protect expectancy (DT-SL-188/039/041).

phases.name: Pre-Market Mapping
phases.goal: Build an initial hypothesis from structure + key levels + zone + scenarios (not certainty).

phases.name: Opening Adaptation
phases.goal: Validate (or reject) the plan using opening behavior; wait on unclear opens; take second opportunity only after structure rebuild.

phases.name: Intelligence Engines
phases.goal: Decide the highest-probability side using validity + comfort + liquidity layers + flow + psychology stack.

phases.name: Execution + Management
phases.goal: Execute only after confirmation; manage with strict risk, early profit capture, and exit fast on mismatch or operator exit.

phases.name: Review + Learning
phases.goal: Treat losses as feedback; identify wrong assumptions (driver/zone/liquidity readiness) and update the next plan.

rules.name: Market Structure State (HH/HL vs LH/LL)
rules.condition: Maintain swing highs/lows on the chosen operating timeframe and evaluate swings primarily using candle body closes (not wick extremes).
rules.interpretation: HH+HL implies bullish structure; LH+LL implies bearish structure; mixed structure implies range/transition risk.
rules.action: Use structure state to form the initial hypothesis and to rank setups (trend-following higher confidence than counter-trend).

rules.name: Break of Structure (Continuation vs Failure)
rules.condition: Price closes beyond the most recent swing high/low and then shows acceptance (holds/continues rather than instantly reclaiming back).
rules.interpretation: A clean break + acceptance supports continuation in that direction; immediate reclaim suggests trap or failed break.
rules.action: Trade in the direction of accepted breaks; treat failed breaks as trap candidates and require additional confirmation before entering.

rules.name: Trend Change Requires Confirmation (CHoCH)
rules.condition: A trend-structure breaks opposite to the prevailing structure (e.g., bullish structure breaks a key swing low).
rules.interpretation: One break can be a stop-sweep; trend change is more reliable only after follow-through + new swing formation.
rules.action: Do not flip bias on the first break alone; require confirmation via acceptance + subsequent lower-high (bearish) or higher-low (bullish).

rules.name: Structure-First Level Interpretation
rules.condition: Key levels exist (PDH/PDL/round numbers) while a structure hypothesis is defined.
rules.interpretation: Levels behave as support/resistance relative to structure intent; the same level can flip role after an accepted break.
rules.action: Interpret levels dynamically with structure (aligns with DT-SL-027). Re-evaluate the level role after any accepted break.

rules.name: Psychological Trap Close (Range-Middle Close Near Key Level)
rules.condition: Previous session closes near a key level (PDH/PDL/round number) after multiple rejections above and below; final close is in the middle of the range.
rules.interpretation: Both buyers and sellers hesitate; smart money can accumulate positions without revealing direction.
rules.action: Expect a directional move next session but do not assume breakout direction. Use this as a 'setup context' only.

rules.name: Opening Candle Confirmation (Gap + First Candle)
rules.condition: Use MATRIX-OPEN-001 at/after first candle completes.
rules.interpretation: Opening behavior provides an early confirmation or a trap signal.
rules.action: If matrix says 'trap/volatile', avoid or wait for structure clarity.

rules.name: No SL = No Move (Low Liquidity Fuel)
rules.condition: After a large move, there are no obvious nearby recent highs/lows or SL clusters.
rules.interpretation: Smart money lacks liquidity fuel; strong one-way continuation is less likely immediately.
rules.action: Expect consolidation/fake moves. Avoid aggressive entries; prefer waiting for new liquidity creation or clearer structure.

rules.name: Avoid Direct Gap Trade
rules.condition: Market opens with a gap (gap up or gap down).
rules.interpretation: Immediate trades at open are higher risk due to traps/volatility.
rules.action: Do not trade immediately in the gap direction. Wait for pullback, reclaim failure/success, or structure confirmation.

rules.name: Dual Plan Model (Trend + Liquidity Counter-Plan)
rules.condition: Before market open (or before first trade idea) create two plans.
rules.interpretation: Institutional approach: prepare trend-following and counter-trend scenarios based on liquidity availability and traps.
rules.action: Plan A: trade with Dow bias. Plan B: trade against bias only when SL cluster + trap logic supports it. Choose one after validation.

rules.name: Last 15–30 Minute Momentum Mapping
rules.condition: Analyze last 15–30 minutes of previous session for trend strength, rejection, sideways behavior, and close quality.
rules.interpretation: Often defines next session momentum and reveals late-session positioning.
rules.action: Use as a primary bias input and as a filter for morning setups.

rules.name: Directional Support/Resistance (Dynamic Levels)
rules.condition: A directional bias exists (bullish/bearish).
rules.interpretation: Levels are interpreted through intent: the market respects levels aligned with bias more reliably.
rules.action: If bearish bias: prioritize resistances and ignore weak supports. If bullish bias: prioritize supports and ignore weak resistances.

rules.name: Market Validation Rule (Expectation vs Reality)
rules.condition: Expected behavior differs from actual behavior (e.g., expect selling but market moves up).
rules.interpretation: Your hypothesis is wrong or early.
rules.action: Cancel bias, avoid trade, or rebuild plan based on new structure. Market is always right.

rules.name: Option Premium Bias (Secondary Confirmation)
rules.condition: PUT premiums rising implies bearish pressure; CALL premiums rising implies bullish pressure.
rules.interpretation: Options can reflect positioning/hedging pressure.
rules.action: Use as secondary confirmation only (never primary). If it contradicts price action, defer to price action.

rules.name: Plan Invalid -> No Trade
rules.condition: Opening behavior contradicts the prepared plan or the plan invalidates in real time.
rules.interpretation: Forcing entries increases loss frequency on trap days.
rules.action: Do not force entry; wait for new structure/hypothesis.

rules.name: Delayed Entry Advantage
rules.condition: Early session is volatile/unclear or conflicts with plan.
rules.interpretation: Missing the first trade is acceptable; later clarity improves expectancy.
rules.action: Prefer waiting for clearer windows (e.g., after initial chop) over forced open trades.

rules.name: Profit-Holding Participants Reduce Immediate SL Availability
rules.condition: Many participants are already in profit and holding positions.
rules.interpretation: Stop losses are not easily triggered; price may stall or mean-revert.
rules.action: Expect slower movement or reversal; trade smaller or wait for fresh liquidity creation.

rules.name: Exact Level Reaction
rules.condition: Key levels are identified (PDH/PDL/round numbers/known reaction points).
rules.interpretation: Price frequently reacts at precise numbers rather than wide zones.
rules.action: Use exact level prices for triggers/invalidation rather than broad ranges (while keeping risk controls).

rules.name: Controlled Multi-Entry Sizing
rules.condition: Multiple entries are part of a planned setup.
rules.interpretation: Risk must decrease after each failed attempt.
rules.action: Limit max entries (e.g., 3–4 overall, and see DT-SL-039 for per-idea limit). Reduce size after each failed entry.

rules.name: Time Uncertainty (SL Hunt May Delay 1–2 Sessions)
rules.condition: A clear SL cluster is identified.
rules.interpretation: Market may target it later, not immediately; consolidation can occur.
rules.action: Do not force a trade just because an SL cluster exists. Require readiness/confirmation.

rules.name: Path Uncertainty (Opposite Move Before Real Move)
rules.condition: SL level identified, but path is trap-heavy or liquidity is insufficient.
rules.interpretation: Price may go opposite first to create/collect liquidity, then move to target.
rules.action: Expect early stop-outs; keep SL tight; do not widen SL to 'make it work'.

rules.name: Correct Bias != Correct Entry
rules.condition: Bias is correct but entry timing is wrong.
rules.interpretation: Stop-out can occur before the correct move.
rules.action: Allow re-entry only under strict attempt limits and only after re-validation.

rules.name: SL Creation Phase (Trap Then Real Move)
rules.condition: Market lacks sufficient SL clusters to fuel a clean move.
rules.interpretation: Market may first create new buyers/sellers (fresh SL), then hunt them later.
rules.action: Treat the first breakout/breakdown as potentially a trap until liquidity confirms consumption.

rules.name: Max Attempt Rule (Per Idea)
rules.condition: A single hypothesis/idea is being traded (same SL target logic).
rules.interpretation: Over-attempting a single idea increases losses on delay/path-uncertainty days.
rules.action: Max 1–2 attempts per idea. If not triggered, stop trading that idea.

rules.name: Controlled Loss Acceptance
rules.condition: Small loss occurs due to time/path uncertainty or trap behavior.
rules.interpretation: Loss is part of SL hunting and probabilistic execution.
rules.action: Accept small losses; avoid revenge trading; re-enter only with re-validation and within attempt limits.

rules.name: No Averaging (No Adding to Losers)
rules.condition: A trade is losing in an SL-hunting context.
rules.interpretation: Market may delay or invalidate the idea; averaging destroys capital.
rules.action: Never average losing positions. Exit and re-evaluate instead.

rules.name: Fake Confirmation Is a Cost (Not a System Error)
rules.condition: A high-quality setup triggers with confirmation (reclaim/acceptance/CHoCH), but price reverses quickly and hits SL (a fake move).
rules.interpretation: Markets can spoof common confirmations. Real vs fake is often only obvious after the move; therefore the system is probabilistic.
rules.action: Treat fake moves as expected operating cost: keep SL strict, never widen, never average, and allow re-entry only after re-validation and within attempt limits (DT-SL-036/037/039/041). Maintain positive expectancy by cutting losers fast and letting real moves pay larger (DT-SL-088/067).

rules.name: Round-Number Wall (1–2 Point Defense Trap)
rules.condition: Price repeatedly approaches a major round number (e.g., 26,000) and gets rejected 1–2 points below it multiple times in the first 5–30 minutes.
rules.interpretation: Operator/large participants are defending the round number (a liquidity/comfort wall). This often baits breakout buyers into comfort and then hunts their SL downward.
rules.action: Do not buy the first 'almost-breakout'. Mark the defended band as a trap zone. Prefer: (A) short after a failed attempt + structure shift (CHoCH) or (B) wait for a clean acceptance above the wall (DT-SL-190) before any long.

rules.name: Strong Reclaim/Acceptance Threshold (Avoid 1-Tick Traps)
rules.condition: Considering an entry based on a reclaim/acceptance at a key level (PDH/PDL/round number) in a high-trap environment (comfort, opening volatility, repeated rejections).
rules.interpretation: Micro reclaims (1–2 points/ticks) are frequently spoofed. A stronger reclaim/acceptance reduces fake entries at the cost of worse price.
rules.action: Require 'strong acceptance' on the operating timeframe: price must reclaim by a buffer (configurable) AND hold/continue (e.g., 2–3 consecutive 1m closes beyond level, or one clean 5m body close beyond). If acceptance is not strong, treat as trap/WAIT.

rules.name: Deep Gap Regime (Below/Above PD Levels)
rules.condition: Open occurs far beyond previous day's key levels (e.g., open below PDL or above PDH by a meaningful distance).
rules.interpretation: Deep gaps create panic/comfort quickly and increase trap risk. Early chasing often becomes liquidity.
rules.action: Primary plan: wait for sweep + reclaim or a clear no-pullback trend day confirmation (DT-SL-132). Shorts after a deep gap down require proof of trend-day refusal to bounce; longs after deep gap down require reclaim/CHoCH + acceptance (DT-SL-190).

rules.name: Swing-Based SL Placement (Respect Sweep Low/High)
rules.condition: Entering after a sweep/reclaim or after a structure shift around a key liquidity level.
rules.interpretation: Stops that sit inside the sweep/consolidation are easy to harvest. The logical invalidation is beyond the sweep extreme or the structure pivot, not an arbitrary number.
rules.action: Place SL beyond the sweep low/high (or beyond the structure pivot) with size adjusted accordingly (DT-SL-053). Do not shrink SL into noise; do not widen after entry.

rules.name: OI Walls as Comfort Boundaries (Secondary)
rules.condition: Option-chain OI shows clear 'mega' call/put walls (e.g., strongest call OI above and strongest put OI below), especially when aligned with round numbers.
rules.interpretation: OI walls often mark where the crowd feels safe (writers positioned). Price may range within walls until a trap breaks one side, causing covering.
rules.action: Use OI walls only as secondary context: treat call walls as overhead comfort/resistance zones and put walls as support/comfort zones. Prefer SL-hunt setups that push beyond a wall and then reclaim back inside (trap), or accept above/below with strong acceptance (DT-SL-190).

rules.name: Micro-Sweep / Precision Stop-Hunt (0–2 Point Break)
rules.condition: Price repeatedly breaks a highly obvious level (round number, day low/high, prior swing) by a tiny amount (e.g., 0–2 points) and quickly reclaims back above/below.
rules.interpretation: This is often liquidity engineering: the operator is collecting stops placed at obvious numbers without committing to a full breakdown/breakout.
rules.action: Do not place SL exactly at obvious levels. If trading a reclaim, either (A) place SL beyond the micro-sweep extreme (DT-SL-192) with reduced size (DT-SL-053), or (B) wait for strong acceptance away from the level (DT-SL-190) before entry.

rules.name: Multi-Sweep Range (Tightening -> Breakout Risk)
rules.condition: Multiple micro-sweeps occur at one edge of a range (e.g., repeated lows) while highs compress (lower highs) or vice-versa.
rules.interpretation: A tightening range is forming. Price can remain choppy while stops are harvested, then break violently once liquidity is sufficient.
rules.action: Reduce attempts and avoid trading inside the middle. Prefer waiting for: (A) edge sweep + strong reclaim (DT-SL-190/194) for reversal, or (B) clean break + acceptance (DT-DOW-002) for continuation. If unsure, WAIT (DT-SL-031/187).

rules.name: Obvious-Stop Buffer Rule (Pre-Defined)
rules.condition: Choosing SL placement near obvious reference points (round numbers, day low/high, prior swing, VWAP/value boundaries).
rules.interpretation: Many traders cluster stops at the exact obvious number; micro-sweeps are common even when direction later works.
rules.action: Pre-define an 'obvious-stop buffer' (configurable) and place SL beyond the cluster, not on it. Compensate by lowering size. Never widen SL after entry (DT-SL-041).

rules.name: OI Is Contracts (Proxy), Not Literal Positions
rules.condition: Interpreting option-chain OI levels for bias/support/resistance.
rules.interpretation: OI reflects open contracts (buyer + seller matched). It is a proxy for positioning, not a guarantee of direction or a direct map of who is 'right'.
rules.action: Use OI only as secondary context (DT-SL-193/029). Always confirm with chart structure + price reaction before trading.

rules.name: Strike Selection Filter (5–7 Strikes, Real Volume Premiums)
rules.condition: Scanning the option chain for meaningful OI levels.
rules.interpretation: Very high-premium strikes often have poor bid/ask participation; very low-premium strikes often represent hedges. Both can distort OI interpretation.
rules.action: Focus on ~5–7 strikes above and below the live price where option premiums have real volume/participation (avoid extreme illiquid premiums and very cheap hedging premiums). Build OI levels from this 'active band'.

rules.name: OI Levels = Probabilistic Boundaries (Not Instant Support/Resistance)
rules.condition: Large call OI or put OI exists at a strike near current price.
rules.interpretation: Call-writer heavy strikes often behave like overhead boundaries; put-writer heavy strikes often behave like lower boundaries. But these can be broken temporarily during SL hunts and comfort creation.
rules.action: Use OI strikes as boundary candidates and combine with SL hunting: prefer trades that sweep beyond an OI boundary and reclaim (trap) or show strong acceptance (DT-SL-190).

rules.name: Change-in-OI Interpretation (Build vs Unwind)
rules.condition: Comparing CE/PE OI and change-in-OI around key strikes.
rules.interpretation: Rising OI can mean writing/building; falling OI can mean unwinding/covering. Relative CE vs PE build/unwind provides bias hints but can be noisy.
rules.action: Use MATRIX-OI-001 as a secondary bias input. If OI bias contradicts price action/structure, defer to price action (DT-SL-028).

rules.name: Weekly vs Monthly Alignment (Monthly Dominates Major Trend)
rules.condition: Weekly expiry OI suggests one direction while monthly expiry OI suggests another.
rules.interpretation: Weekly can drive short-term swings, but monthly positioning often dominates the larger swing and limits extremes (range or sideways-to-directional behavior).
rules.action: If weekly and monthly conflict, expect sideways-to-directional (swings + traps). Trade smaller, target partials, and prioritize confirmation. If both align, trend probability increases.

rules.name: Writer Premium Cushion (When 'Support' Can Break)
rules.condition: Put-writer (support) strike shows very high OI, but price still breaks below it intraday.
rules.interpretation: Writers can tolerate adverse price move until the option premium expands back toward their effective sell premium ('pain threshold'). Therefore high put OI does not guarantee immediate defense; the market can break the level first (often as an SL hunt) and recover later.
rules.action: Do not buy solely because put OI is high. Wait for either (A) sweep+reclaim, or (B) evidence that premium expansion is forcing defense (writer discomfort). Combine with chart confirmation and strong acceptance (DT-SL-190).

rules.name: OI Ambiguity -> Chart First (Fallback Rule)
rules.condition: CE/PE OI looks balanced/unclear, or change-in-OI is mixed, or expiry context makes OI interpretation noisy (holiday/event risk).
rules.interpretation: Option chain alone may not reveal direction; forcing an OI-based trade increases fake signals.
rules.action: Fallback to chart engines: Dow structure + zone + liquidity + opening matrix. Use OI only as boundary context (DT-SL-199/193) after chart bias exists.

rules.name: Writer Comfort vs Pressure (Premium Expansion Heuristic)
rules.condition: Interpreting whether writers will defend a strike immediately during a move into/through an OI wall.
rules.interpretation: Writers often tolerate small underlying breaches when premium remains low (comfortable). Defense becomes more likely when premium expands quickly (pressured).
rules.action: Classify premium_state using MATRIX-OI-002. If comfortable, expect fake breaks and use sweep+reclaim logic. If pressured, expect faster reactions and treat the wall as more active; still require strong acceptance/confirmation (DT-SL-190).

rules.name: Sub-Round Levels (200/800) Become Active
rules.condition: Market repeatedly reacts around sub-round levels (e.g., 46,200 / 46,800) in addition to 0/50/100 round numbers.
rules.interpretation: As positioning shifts, new 'active' magnets appear. Traders adapt; operators often defend/attack these sub-round nodes, creating repeatable fake breaks and rejections.
rules.action: Include 200/800 levels as key intraday nodes when you observe repeated reactions. Treat them like round numbers: expect micro-sweeps, place SL beyond obvious clusters (DT-SL-194/196), and demand strong acceptance for true breaks (DT-SL-190).

rules.name: Weekly Expiry Start Anchor (Infer Writers via First Post-Expiry Reaction)
rules.condition: A new weekly expiry cycle starts and price quickly forms a pivot (support/resistance) near a key strike/level.
rules.interpretation: Early-cycle pivots often reflect fresh writer positioning; that anchor can shape the week's range.
rules.action: Mark the first clear weekly-cycle pivot level as an anchor. Use it as a boundary in scenario planning (gap up/down/flat) and expect fake breaks around it unless strong acceptance appears (DT-SL-190).

rules.name: Trap Classification First (Trade the Trap Type, Not the Candle)
rules.condition: Market behavior feels confusing or contradictory (breaks fail, fast reversals, no follow-through, or repeated stop-outs near obvious levels).
rules.interpretation: Different trap regimes require different execution: sideways traps punish impatience; volatile whipsaw traps punish tight stops and overtrading; breakout/breakdown failures punish chasing; news traps invalidate normal assumptions.
rules.action: Before the next attempt, classify trap_type using MATRIX-TRAP-001. Then apply the trap-specific action and reduce attempts/size until structure + acceptance is clear (DT-SL-039/187/190).

rules.name: Sideways Trap (Range Farming)
rules.condition: Price remains inside a range with low momentum; both sides wait for a big breakout/breakdown; repeated small fake moves occur.
rules.interpretation: The market farms time and impatience. Traders lose via churn, fees, and multiple small stop-outs, even when the eventual breakout is real.
rules.action: Avoid the middle. Either (A) trade only edges after sweep+reclaim confirmation (DT-SL-190/194), or (B) wait for clean break + acceptance away from the range (DT-DOW-002). Keep size small and cap attempts to 1 per side.

rules.name: Volatile Whipsaw Trap (Fast Up-Down, No One-Direction Follow-Through)
rules.condition: Large candles alternate directions quickly; moves retrace rapidly; both buyers and sellers get stopped repeatedly.
rules.interpretation: Volatility is being used as a trap tool. Microstructure becomes noisy; tight 'perfect' stops are easy targets; chasing becomes instant liquidity.
rules.action: Reduce size and frequency. Do not add/pyramid. Prefer WAIT 5-15 minutes to rebuild structure on 5m and re-enter only after acceptance/reclaim is clear (DT-SL-148/121/190). If whipsaw persists, stop trading for the session (DT-SL-049/187).

rules.name: Post-Break Trap (Breakout/Breakdown Failure After Trigger)
rules.condition: A breakout/breakdown triggers obvious participation, but price fails to accept beyond the level and quickly reclaims back inside.
rules.interpretation: The break was used to create fresh SL pools (new participants). Failure converts those participants into liquidity, often accelerating the opposite move.
rules.action: Do not chase the break. Trade only after failure confirmation: (A) breakout failure -> look for sell after failed acceptance + reclaim below the level; (B) breakdown failure -> look for buy after sweep + reclaim above the level. Use the broken level as a pivot and place SL beyond micro-sweep extremes (DT-SL-194/196).

rules.name: Fear/Greed Trap Filter (Mindset-Driven Losses)
rules.condition: You hesitate to buy because price looks 'too high' after multiple green candles, or you feel urgency to chase a breakout/breakdown to not miss the move.
rules.interpretation: These are common mindset traps: fear leads to early counter-trend sells; greed leads to late entries that become liquidity.
rules.action: No impulse entries. Follow the rulebook triggers only: zone alignment (MATRIX-ZONE-001), liquidity evidence, and confirmation (DT-SL-190/073). If you missed the move, skip and wait for the next setup (DT-SL-173).

rules.name: Regime Flip After Multi-Day One-Side Move (Which Break Fails Changes)
rules.condition: Market has moved strongly for 3-5 sessions in one direction and then prints a 'final' breakout/breakdown close that makes the direction look obvious to the crowd.
rules.interpretation: Psychology saturates: after multi-day selling, sellers crowd in and the next trap often becomes a breakdown-failure (seller trap). After multi-day buying, buyers crowd in and the next trap often becomes a breakout-failure (buyer trap).
rules.action: Next session, be alert for the opposite trap type: after multi-day sell + breakdown close -> prioritize breakdown-failure long setups (sweep below + reclaim). After multi-day buy + breakout close -> prioritize breakout-failure short setups (fake break + reclaim back under). Require acceptance/confirmation; do not pre-commit (DT-SL-147/183).

rules.name: Sudden Capital Injection (Operator Push)
rules.condition: Market shows accumulation/slow build behavior.
rules.interpretation: Operator can inject large capital suddenly, triggering fast directional movement.
rules.action: If accumulation + sudden expansion appears, expect momentum continuation and SL cascade risk; manage entries and SL accordingly.

rules.name: Chain Reaction (SL Cascade)
rules.condition: Initial move hits small SLs, causing price acceleration.
rules.interpretation: Momentum can be a liquidity chain reaction rather than organic trend.
rules.action: On cascade behavior, avoid fading blindly; prefer trading with cascade only when structure supports it.

rules.name: New Liquidity Creation (Break -> New Participants -> Fresh SL)
rules.condition: A breakout/breakdown occurs at a key level.
rules.interpretation: New traders enter and create fresh SL pools; liquidity map changes dynamically.
rules.action: Track both old SL and newly created SL after breaks; re-evaluate target selection accordingly.

rules.name: Profit Holder Trap (Future Liquidity)
rules.condition: Participants are in profit and holding instead of exiting.
rules.interpretation: These positions can become future liquidity targets (future SL/forced exits).
rules.action: Include profit-holder positioning in liquidity map; avoid assuming immediate continuation without fresh liquidity.

rules.name: Level Break Liquidity Shift
rules.condition: A key level breaks (e.g., round number like 50,000).
rules.interpretation: Participants enter in the direction of the break; SL pools form quickly around the level.
rules.action: Treat the break as a liquidity shift event; update SL map and avoid late chasing without a pullback/structure.

rules.name: Partial Correctness Failure (Missing Liquidity Layer)
rules.condition: Support/trend/structure analysis is correct but trade fails.
rules.interpretation: Failure can come from missing liquidity creation/participant layer rather than wrong structure.
rules.action: When stopped out, re-check liquidity map (old/new/profit-holders) before retrying the same idea.

rules.name: Market Compatibility Principle
rules.condition: Strategy performs well only in certain market phases (trend/cascade/accumulation).
rules.interpretation: Some days are decoding-match; some days are mismatch.
rules.action: Continuously evaluate compatibility using DT-SL-049. If mismatch, stop trading.

rules.name: Decoding Mismatch Day -> Stop Trading
rules.condition: Multiple consecutive plan failures, unpredictable whipsaws, or repeated invalidations early in session.
rules.interpretation: Your decoding logic is mismatched to the day's operator behavior/phase.
rules.action: Exit fast and stop trading for the day (or reduce to observation-only mode).

rules.name: Gradual Build -> Sudden Move
rules.condition: Slow accumulation/consolidation precedes a sharp expansion candle/impulse.
rules.interpretation: Operator behavior: build quietly then push suddenly (aligns with velocity logic).
rules.action: Combine with opening and liquidity phase checks; avoid entering late after the sudden push without pullback/structure.

rules.name: Risk Zone Targeting (Zones, Not Individuals)
rules.condition: Mapping SL-hunting targets for the next move.
rules.interpretation: Markets often target common risk zones where many stops sit (breakouts/breakdowns/obvious entries), not individual traders.
rules.action: Map the crowd's likely entry zones and typical stop zones around them; treat zones as probabilistic targets and still require confirmation before entries.

rules.name: Retail Predictability (Common Entries + Fixed SL Distances)
rules.condition: Retail participation is present and breakouts/breakdowns are being attempted repeatedly.
rules.interpretation: Retail entries and stop distances are often clustered and predictable, creating dense SL pools.
rules.action: Use a configurable proxy for typical SL distances (e.g., ~100–200 BankNifty points) around obvious breakout/breakdown levels and round numbers; validate with swing clusters and reaction behavior.

rules.name: Adaptive SL Range (Pre-Defined; Never Widen After Entry)
rules.condition: Tight SL entries are repeatedly noise-stopped or entry precision is low in the current phase.
rules.interpretation: Tight SL can be hunted, but widening SL after entry is destructive and usually narrative-driven.
rules.action: Choose one: (A) improve entry precision and keep SL tight (preferred), or (B) pre-define a slightly wider SL with smaller size. Never widen SL after entry (DT-SL-036/041).

rules.name: Big Money Dominance (Flow > Retail Excitement)
rules.condition: Retail excitement (obvious breakout buying/selling) conflicts with operator/flow signals or liquidity logic.
rules.interpretation: Sustained trend follows larger capital direction; retail flow alone is not proof of continuation.
rules.action: Weight structure + operator behavior + liquidity flow higher than retail excitement. Treat retail-only breakouts as trap candidates until confirmed by acceptance/structure and validity filters.

rules.name: Range SL Farming (Both Sides Repeatedly Trapped)
rules.condition: Higher-TF structure is mixed/ranging and breaks repeatedly fail (false breakouts/breakdowns).
rules.interpretation: Range markets harvest SL on both sides; breakout chasing becomes low expectancy.
rules.action: Avoid chasing range breaks. Prefer sweep+reclaim, failed acceptance, and liquidity-target setups near range edges; apply fair-zone veto by default (DT-SL-141).

rules.name: Repeated Outcome Bias (Follow the Side That Keeps Working)
rules.condition: Recent sessions repeatedly reward one side (e.g., downside moves pay quickly; pullbacks fail; puts work consistently).
rules.interpretation: Positioning/phase can favor one side for a period; fighting it increases stop-outs.
rules.action: Use repeated outcomes as a bias weight (not a trigger). Reduce contrarian attempts against the dominant side unless trap confirmation is exceptional.

rules.name: Trend Transition Warning (Edge Degrades)
rules.condition: Consecutive losses, repeated invalidations, or your usual high-quality conditions stop working.
rules.interpretation: Market phase may be transitioning; your decoding becomes unreliable.
rules.action: Reduce size/frequency, shift to observation-first, and use mismatch kill switch if instability persists (DT-SL-049).

rules.name: Direction Dependency (Pattern Needs Correct Bias)
rules.condition: A familiar pattern appears but directional confluence is missing or psychology contradicts it.
rules.interpretation: Patterns lose expectancy when bias/positioning is wrong; the same pattern can fail repeatedly in a phase shift.
rules.action: Use patterns only after bias selection (structure + liquidity + psychology) and price confirmation (DT-SL-073). If direction is unclear, wait.

rules.name: Intraday Integrity (Do Not Convert to Positional)
rules.condition: You feel tempted to hold an intraday trade longer due to hope or 'it will come back'.
rules.interpretation: Positional holding without thesis destroys intraday expectancy and increases drawdown.
rules.action: Follow invalidation exits strictly (DT-SL-185). Never convert intraday trades into positional holds.

rules.name: Emotion Distortion Triggers (Profit -> Greed, Loss -> Fear)
rules.condition: After a win/loss, you feel urgency to trade bigger, average, or revenge trade.
rules.interpretation: Emotion distorts execution and turns you into liquidity (overtrading, holding losers, averaging).
rules.action: Enforce cool-down and strict attempt limits (DT-SL-039). Never average losers (DT-SL-041). Stop early on mismatch signals (DT-SL-049).

rules.name: Psychology -> Trend Formation
rules.condition: Setting bias before clear trend is visible.
rules.interpretation: Trend is often an outcome of psychology shift + forced risk transfer (trapped participants) rather than the starting point.
rules.action: Map who is trapped/confident/at risk and where their exits/SLs sit; expect trend to form after liquidity is created/consumed. Validate with structure + confirmation before execution.

rules.name: One-Sided Market -> Forced Opposite Move (Create Liquidity)
rules.condition: Only one side is active (only buyers or only sellers) and the opposite side is absent/scared (DT-SL-162).
rules.interpretation: One-sided conditions often stall or force an opposite move to create participation and new SL liquidity.
rules.action: Avoid chasing one-sided breakouts/breakdowns. Wait for two-sided participation or expect pullback/sweep behavior before the real move.

rules.name: Crowd Imbalance Principle (High Crowd -> Low Profit)
rules.condition: Crowd participation becomes obvious (comfort cluster / crowded alignment).
rules.interpretation: Where the crowd is highest, edge is often lowest; trap probability increases.
rules.action: Avoid entering at peak crowd comfort. Prefer post-trap entries after confirmation (DT-SL-103..110) or trade the dominant side earlier only with strong validity.

rules.name: Psychology Over Chart/News (When Positioning Conflicts)
rules.condition: News/chart looks strongly bullish/bearish but positioning is one-sided and comfort is extreme.
rules.interpretation: News and obvious chart patterns can be used to attract the crowd; positioning/psychology can dominate direction next.
rules.action: Do not trade purely on news or obvious breakout. Require liquidity + confirmation; allow contrarian bias only after trap evidence appears.

rules.name: Risk Holder Identification (Gap Up Buyers-at-Risk, Gap Down Sellers-at-Risk)
rules.condition: Market opens with a gap relative to previous close.
rules.interpretation: The open defines initial trapped/risk holders and shapes early liquidity targeting.
rules.action: Identify who is initially at risk (gap up -> buyers; gap down -> sellers). Prefer trading against trapped risk holders after confirmation (failed acceptance, sweep+reclaim, rejection).

rules.name: Turning Point Detection (Sudden Reversal = Strong Operator Entry)
rules.condition: After a move, a sudden reversal/impulse appears (sharp candle + follow-through).
rules.interpretation: Operator entry can flip psychology quickly and start a new trend leg or continuation.
rules.action: Treat as a high-signal event: wait for structure shift + acceptance/reclaim, then align with the new direction; avoid immediate fading without proof.

rules.name: Intraday Target Discipline (Default ~1:1 Capture)
rules.condition: Managing targets in intraday SL-hunting trades.
rules.interpretation: Overholding increases giveback and trap risk; consistent partial capture improves expectancy.
rules.action: Default: take first profit near ~1:1 RR or a nearby liquidity objective. Hold remainder only if holding/continuation evidence persists (DT-SL-088/165).

rules.name: Gap Distance Invalidation (Very Large Gaps)
rules.condition: Gap is very large (e.g., ~300–400 BankNifty points) or extreme vs recent volatility.
rules.interpretation: Prior micro-structure and levels can lose relevance; a new intraday chart forms quickly.
rules.action: Apply large-gap discipline: treat as new chart (DT-SL-163), rebuild structure in first 10–20 minutes, and avoid aggressive early trades.

rules.name: Contrarian Edge (Trade Against Crowd After Comfort)
rules.condition: Crowd is committed/comfortable (obvious breakout, alignment, heavy participation).
rules.interpretation: Crowd comfort creates dense SL pools and can become the next target.
rules.action: Do not pre-empt. Prefer contrarian only after trap confirmation (failed break, sweep+reclaim, operator exit). Enforce strict invalidation + attempt limits.

rules.name: Multi-Factor Psychology Stack (Bias Selector)
rules.condition: Selecting directional bias for the session/day.
rules.interpretation: Single-factor bias is fragile; higher confidence comes from stacked evidence.
rules.action: Score bias using: Dow structure, opening context (gap + first candle), liquidity layers (old/new/profit-holders), risk holders (DT-SL-065), crowd comfort/overcrowding, and optional OI as secondary confirmation. Execute only if stack aligns + price confirms (DT-SL-073).

rules.name: Dynamic Plan Switching
rules.condition: An initial plan exists, but live price action invalidates it or shows strong opposite behavior.
rules.interpretation: A pre-decided plan is dangerous; the market-driven plan has priority.
rules.action: Switch bias (or go flat) immediately, rebuild hypothesis from structure + liquidity, and re-enter only after confirmation.

rules.name: No Fixed Formula
rules.condition: You are about to trade using a rigid mapping (e.g., 'gap up always sell' or 'breakout always buy').
rules.interpretation: Rigid formulas ignore market context and operator intent.
rules.action: Do not auto-trade. Require context + confirmation (structure/liquidity/price behavior) before any entry.

rules.name: Market Confirmation Before Entry
rules.condition: A trade idea exists based on bias/liquidity/psychology.
rules.interpretation: Prediction without confirmation is low quality in live markets.
rules.action: Enter only after price confirms (e.g., reclaim + hold, rejection + continuation, or structure break + acceptance).

rules.name: Wait vs Early Entry Trade-off
rules.condition: Choosing between early entry (higher RR) vs confirmed entry (higher accuracy).
rules.interpretation: Early entries improve RR but reduce accuracy; confirmed entries reduce RR but improve reliability.
rules.action: Prefer confirmed entries by default. If taking early entry, reduce size and enforce strict invalidation.

rules.name: Pattern Instability Detection
rules.condition: Repeated whipsaw behavior (sell -> recover -> sell -> recover) with no clean acceptance.
rules.interpretation: Unstable structure; high trap risk.
rules.action: Reduce size, tighten risk, avoid aggressive bias, or shift to observation-only until structure stabilizes.

rules.name: Round Number Activation
rules.condition: Price is near a major round number (e.g., 42000 / 50000).
rules.interpretation: Round numbers attract participation; breaks often create new liquidity and faster reactions.
rules.action: Treat breaks as liquidity shift events; avoid late chasing and prefer pullback/confirmation entries.

rules.name: Multi-Index Alignment (NIFTY + BANKNIFTY)
rules.condition: Trading index direction intraday.
rules.interpretation: Alignment increases conviction; divergence increases trap risk.
rules.action: If both align, confidence increases. If divergence, reduce size or wait for clearer confirmation.

rules.name: Technical + Psychology Hybrid Entry
rules.condition: A psychological narrative exists but needs execution trigger.
rules.interpretation: Psychology alone can be ambiguous; technical triggers reduce subjectivity.
rules.action: Use a hybrid gate: require (psychology + liquidity) AND a technical trigger (reclaim/rejection/acceptance) before entry.

rules.name: Missed Trade Acceptance
rules.condition: The optimal entry is missed and price moves away.
rules.interpretation: Chasing increases error rate and trap risk.
rules.action: Skip the trade; wait for a new setup or a new pullback-based confirmation.

rules.name: Partial Entry Scaling
rules.condition: A setup is valid but confirmation is still building.
rules.interpretation: Scaling reduces exposure while letting the market prove the move.
rules.action: Enter partial size first; add only after further confirmation, always respecting max risk and attempt limits.

rules.name: Market Effort Principle
rules.condition: You feel the need to 'force' a trade or predict every move.
rules.interpretation: Not everything is the trader's job; let the market confirm and do the work.
rules.action: Wait for confirmation and structure. If market does not confirm, do not trade.

rules.name: Turning Point Entry Detection
rules.condition: A sharp reversal/turning point occurs (often near a key level or after a sweep).
rules.interpretation: Assume operator entry/defense around the turning point (exact entry is not visible).
rules.action: Mark the turning point as an operator zone; prefer trading in alignment once confirmation appears.

rules.name: Unit-Based Capital Deployment
rules.condition: Price turns gradually with repeated small defenses rather than one clean reversal.
rules.interpretation: Operators deploy in phases/units, not a single full-size entry.
rules.action: Expect accumulation/distribution behavior; avoid assuming 'one candle = done'. Use partial entries and confirmation.

rules.name: Operator Hierarchy (Bigger Overrides Smaller)
rules.condition: A move from one side is repeatedly invalidated by a larger, stronger impulse from the opposite side.
rules.interpretation: Larger operators can override smaller ones; dominance can change.
rules.action: Follow the dominant impulse after confirmation; avoid fighting strength based on older bias.

rules.name: Trend = Dominant Operator Holding
rules.condition: Trend continues with clean continuation and no exit signatures.
rules.interpretation: Trend persists while the dominant operator holds positions.
rules.action: Stay with the trend; reduce counter-trend attempts unless exit signals or strong invalidation appears.

rules.name: Operator Exit Pattern
rules.condition: After a strong move, price shifts into slow grind, small pullbacks, reduced follow-through, and choppy progress.
rules.interpretation: Operator may be booking profits / distributing.
rules.action: Tighten stops, take partial profits, avoid fresh late entries, and wait for next confirmation.

rules.name: Post-Exit Reversal Expectation
rules.condition: Operator exit pattern is detected and price stops progressing.
rules.interpretation: After exit, reversal probability increases.
rules.action: Do not reverse immediately; wait for reversal confirmation (reclaim failure, acceptance, or structure shift).

rules.name: Hold With Operator Until Exit
rules.condition: In a trade aligned with operator dominance and trend continuation.
rules.interpretation: Best profits often come from staying with the operator, not micromanaging.
rules.action: Hold while continuation persists; manage using exit signatures (DT-SL-086/093) and invalidation levels.

rules.name: Operator Trap Support/Resistance
rules.condition: Operator selling is suspected while price hovers at an apparent support (or buying at an apparent resistance).
rules.interpretation: The visible level can become a trap; the market may break after building participants.
rules.action: Avoid trusting support/resistance blindly; require confirmation and check for liquidity creation behavior.

rules.name: Expiry Day Behavior
rules.condition: Index/options expiry day or adjacent sessions.
rules.interpretation: Forced exits and positioning effects can distort normal SL/structure behavior; next-day behavior may reverse.
rules.action: Reduce risk and avoid strong assumptions; prioritize confirmation and consider stopping early if volatility is abnormal.

rules.name: Event Timing Edge (Expiry/Holidays/Major Events)
rules.condition: Low participation windows (holidays) or major event risk (news/global factors).
rules.interpretation: Operators can exploit low participation; behavior can become non-standard.
rules.action: Classify the day type early; reduce size, tighten limits, or avoid trading if event-driven.

rules.name: Delayed Exit Validation
rules.condition: You suspect operator exit but confirmation is unclear intraday.
rules.interpretation: Exit is often confirmed only after the next reaction (sometimes next session).
rules.action: Avoid aggressive reversal trades without confirmation; use conservative management and wait for structure shift.

rules.name: Exit vs Continuation Read
rules.condition: Comparing strong impulse behavior vs choppy incremental behavior after a move.
rules.interpretation: Strong move with minimal pullback suggests holding; choppy micro-moves suggest exit/distribution.
rules.action: If holding signatures: stay with trend. If exit signatures: tighten stops/scale out and stop chasing.

rules.name: SL Hunting Effectiveness Filter
rules.condition: Before applying SL-hunting logic for a trade idea.
rules.interpretation: SL hunting is not universal; effectiveness depends on participation and liquidity availability.
rules.action: Proceed only if fresh participants are entering AND SL clusters are available AND the day is not strongly event-driven.

rules.name: Don't Target Deeply Profitable Traders
rules.condition: A large portion of participants are already in significant profit.
rules.interpretation: Profitable traders often exit manually; SL liquidity may not exist where you expect.
rules.action: Avoid building targets purely around their assumed SLs; wait for new liquidity creation or different structure.

rules.name: Retail Participation Requirement
rules.condition: Low retail participation / fear-driven avoidance / no fresh entries.
rules.interpretation: Without fresh retail positions, SL hunting has weaker fuel.
rules.action: Reduce SL-hunting reliance; switch to operator + confirmation model or reduce trading frequency.

rules.name: Psychology Can Override Chart
rules.condition: Chart/levels suggest reversal but behavior/flow indicates continuation (or vice versa).
rules.interpretation: In certain conditions, psychology/positioning dominates static chart logic.
rules.action: Weight psychology + operator intent higher than static pattern expectations; demand stronger confirmation before fading.

rules.name: Lagging Market Trap
rules.condition: One index has moved strongly while another lags; retail shifts to the laggard expecting catch-up.
rules.interpretation: Operators can trap participants in the lagging market.
rules.action: Be cautious with laggard breakouts; require multi-index confirmation (DT-SL-077) and strong acceptance before entry.

rules.name: Non-SL Momentum Exists
rules.condition: Strong one-way movement occurs without an obvious SL structure trigger.
rules.interpretation: Move can be event/capital-flow driven rather than SL cascade.
rules.action: Stop forcing an SL narrative; treat as momentum/flow day, reduce counter-trend attempts, and prioritize confirmation.

rules.name: Event-Driven Market Override
rules.condition: Major news, global events, or scheduled high-impact events dominate market behavior.
rules.interpretation: Event days can invalidate typical SL-hunting and structure expectations.
rules.action: Reduce size or avoid trading; if trading, require strict confirmation and tighter daily loss limits.

rules.name: Profit Distribution Mapping
rules.condition: Before selecting targets and applying SL-hunting logic.
rules.interpretation: Knowing who is in profit vs loss changes liquidity expectations.
rules.action: Map likely profit-holders vs trapped participants; use it to adjust SL targets and validity checks.

rules.name: 70-30 Discipline Rule
rules.condition: Recognizing that the model can fail in a meaningful minority of sessions.
rules.interpretation: Overtrading the uncertainty zone causes most drawdowns.
rules.action: Accept uncertainty; cut frequency, enforce attempt limits, and stop early on mismatch signals (DT-SL-049).

rules.name: Comfort Rule (Crowd Comfort -> Opposite Move Risk)
rules.condition: Multiple confirmations align (obvious breakout, indicator agreement, sector/index alignment) and crowd participation increases near the same direction.
rules.interpretation: High comfort zones attract maximum crowd entries and build dense SL pools; reversal/trap probability increases.
rules.action: Do not chase the comfortable direction. Wait for comfort confirmation and then trade only with strict confirmation (often contrarian or post-trap).

rules.name: Comfort Cluster Formation
rules.condition: Index + heavyweight stocks (or multiple correlated instruments) show the same visible breakout/breakdown at the same time.
rules.interpretation: A comfort cluster forms; crowd enters together, creating strong liquidity pools and trap potential.
rules.action: Mark the cluster zone as a high-probability liquidity/trap area; reduce impulse entries and wait for rejection/failed break or post-sweep reclaim.

rules.name: Comfort Timing (Don't Enter Before Comfort)
rules.condition: A setup direction is identified but crowd comfort has not formed yet (participation still hesitant).
rules.interpretation: Entering too early often causes stop-out even when the direction is correct.
rules.action: Wait for the comfort event (obvious breakout / alignment / crowd entry) and then look for trap confirmation before entry.

rules.name: Late (Confirmed) Entry Advantage After Comfort
rules.condition: Comfort is confirmed and the market shows a trap signature (failed break, rejection, sweep + reclaim).
rules.interpretation: Best entries often come after the crowd commits, not during prediction.
rules.action: Prefer confirmed entries after the comfort event even if RR is smaller; use partial entry scaling (DT-SL-080).

rules.name: Comfort Zones Create SL Pools
rules.condition: Comfort cluster forms near a level and price stalls/hesitates.
rules.interpretation: New positions create fresh SL pools around the comfort level.
rules.action: Treat comfort zones as likely SL clusters; integrate with SL mapping (old/new/profit-holder liquidity).

rules.name: Multi-Instrument Comfort Alignment
rules.condition: Comfort signals align across index + key constituents + related index (e.g., NIFTY + BANKNIFTY).
rules.interpretation: Comfort intensity increases; traps can be sharper and reversals faster.
rules.action: Increase trap vigilance; require stronger confirmation for trend-chasing and prefer post-trap entries.

rules.name: Early Entry Failure Despite Correct Idea
rules.condition: You entered before comfort confirmation and got stopped out, then the market moved in your intended direction.
rules.interpretation: Entry timing was wrong, not necessarily the idea.
rules.action: Do not revenge trade. Re-enter only after comfort + confirmation and within attempt limits (DT-SL-039).

rules.name: No SL != No Move (Comfort Creation First)
rules.condition: After a strong sell/buy, visible SL pools appear absent in the opposite direction.
rules.interpretation: Market may still move opposite temporarily to create comfort/fresh participants, then reverse into the true direction.
rules.action: Do not assume 'no SL means no move'. Watch for comfort creation + trap confirmation before committing.

rules.name: Early Profit Exit (Don't Aim Perfection)
rules.condition: Trade moves in your favor and reaches a reasonable objective or shows exit/distribution signatures.
rules.interpretation: Unbooked profit often turns into loss during reversals/traps.
rules.action: Book partial/full profits when available; avoid waiting for perfect targets if structure shows stalling or operator exit (DT-SL-086).

rules.name: SL-Based Continuation Bias
rules.condition: One side entered heavily, got swept/stopped, and the next session opens with a confirming gap in the same direction as the dominant move.
rules.interpretation: Stops provide fuel and continuation becomes more likely when the next open confirms dominance.
rules.action: Build a continuation hypothesis for next day; still require opening confirmation (gap + first candle) and validity filters.

rules.name: Dual Scenario Analysis (Buyers vs Sellers)
rules.condition: Before committing to a bias around a key level/zone.
rules.interpretation: Both sides can be trapped; one-sided thinking increases errors.
rules.action: Always model two scenarios: buyer-trap case and seller-trap case, then let opening/behavior confirm which is active.

rules.name: Time Count Is Not a Reversal Signal
rules.condition: Bias is formed mainly because the market moved up/down for multiple days.
rules.interpretation: Days count does not imply reversal; continuation depends on who exited/trapped and operator holding.
rules.action: Do not fade trend solely due to time count. Require exit/invalidations and confirmation.

rules.name: Seller Exhaustion / Exit Detection
rules.condition: Strong down move followed by sharp retracement that invalidates easy continuation.
rules.interpretation: Sellers may have exited or become trapped; target may shift to buyers.
rules.action: Reduce bearish conviction, look for structure shift/reclaim, and reassess targets using DT-SL-116.

rules.name: Next Target Shift (After One Side Exits)
rules.condition: Evidence suggests one side has exited/been exhausted (e.g., seller exhaustion or buyer exhaustion).
rules.interpretation: Liquidity target often shifts to the opposite side.
rules.action: Update liquidity map and aim next targets toward the opposite side's SL pools (with confirmation).

rules.name: Fear-Based Non-Participation
rules.condition: Market moves fast/erratic and participants hesitate; fresh entries are scarce.
rules.interpretation: Less fresh SL is created; SL hunting becomes less reliable.
rules.action: Reduce frequency/size and favor confirmation-based momentum/operator reads over SL targeting.

rules.name: Emotion Behind Candles
rules.condition: Reading candle sequences around key levels (strong impulse, hesitation, repeated rejection).
rules.interpretation: Candles reflect fear/greed/hesitation; emotion shifts often precede traps and reversals.
rules.action: Use emotion cues as a filter: hesitation near comfort levels increases trap probability; strong acceptance reduces it.

rules.name: Retracement Requirement for Liquidity Creation
rules.condition: A target requires fresh participants/SL to be created before a clean move is likely.
rules.interpretation: Market may retrace first to build comfort and liquidity.
rules.action: Prefer entries after retracement + confirmation rather than chasing the initial impulse.

rules.name: Index Behavior Difference (Analyze Separately)
rules.condition: Comparing signals between indices (e.g., NIFTY vs BANKNIFTY).
rules.interpretation: Different indices can have different phases (range vs trend) simultaneously.
rules.action: Analyze each index independently for structure/phase; use correlation as confirmation, not as substitution.

rules.name: Timeframe Alignment (Execution vs Confirmation)
rules.condition: Selecting timeframes for signal generation and execution.
rules.interpretation: Lower TF shows micro traps and precise SL hits; higher TF reduces noise and improves confirmation clarity.
rules.action: Use 1-min for execution precision and microstructure; use 5-min/15-min for trend/confirmation and hypothesis framing.

rules.name: Price Reaction Over Candle Close
rules.condition: Evaluating SL hunts and traps around breaks/sweeps.
rules.interpretation: Micro reaction (sweep + snapback) can matter more than candle close on higher TF.
rules.action: In SL hunting, prioritize price reaction and immediate reclaim behavior; do not rely only on higher-TF candle closes.

rules.name: Micro Structure Visibility Advantage
rules.condition: You suspect SL sweeps/traps but higher TF candles hide the sequence.
rules.interpretation: Lower TF can reveal the exact sweep, reclaim, and trap sequence.
rules.action: Drop to 1-min to validate SL sweep/reclaim; only then execute with risk controls.

rules.name: Range + Gap Strategy
rules.condition: Market is sideways/ranging on higher TF and opens with a gap.
rules.interpretation: In ranges, gaps often fade into range logic more cleanly than in trends.
rules.action: If ranging: gap up favors longs (buy dips); gap down favors shorts (sell bounces) after confirmation.

rules.name: Trap Before Continuation (Trend + Gap)
rules.condition: A trend exists on higher TF and the market opens with a gap.
rules.interpretation: In trends, the first move after open can trap the wrong side before continuation resumes.
rules.action: In uptrend: expect early drop/trap before continuation. In downtrend: expect early rise/trap before continuation. Trade only after confirmation (DT-SL-073).

rules.name: Morning Trap Capture
rules.condition: First significant move after open conflicts with higher-TF trend or comfort logic.
rules.interpretation: First move can be trap/liq-creation rather than true direction.
rules.action: Avoid impulse entry on first move; wait for trap confirmation (failed break / reclaim) and then execute.

rules.name: Trend-Based Option Side Bias (Secondary)
rules.condition: Using options intraday with an identified higher-TF trend.
rules.interpretation: Opposite-side options can be used during early trap moves (e.g., in uptrend, early drop may reward puts).
rules.action: Treat as secondary execution insight only; do not override price confirmation and risk controls.

rules.name: Gap Size Behavior
rules.condition: Gap size is large vs small relative to recent volatility.
rules.interpretation: Large gaps increase two-sided volatility and trap risk; small gaps can be cleaner.
rules.action: On large gaps, reduce size and require more confirmation; on small gaps, still follow the opening matrix and confirmation rules.

rules.name: No Trend Mode (Follow Flow, Avoid Prediction)
rules.condition: Higher TF has no clear structure/trend (range/transition).
rules.interpretation: Prediction quality drops; traps increase.
rules.action: Follow confirmed flow and microstructure; reduce frequency and avoid strong directional predictions.

rules.name: Timeframe Confidence Rule
rules.condition: You are tempted to trade on a timeframe you are not trained to execute reliably.
rules.interpretation: Mismatch between skill and timeframe increases execution errors.
rules.action: Trade only on trained timeframes; if unsure, step up to confirmation TF and wait.

rules.name: Pre-Market Structure Planning (Timeframe + Gap Expectation)
rules.condition: Before session open.
rules.interpretation: Clear planning improves execution and reduces impulsive decisions.
rules.action: Plan using higher TF structure, key levels, and gap scenarios; execute on 1-min only after confirmation.

rules.name: No-Pullback Trend Day
rules.condition: After open, price shows strong directional move with successive lower highs (bearish) or higher lows (bullish) and no meaningful retracement to prior key levels.
rules.interpretation: Some days are direct momentum days; waiting for an ideal pullback can miss the best move.
rules.action: Trade continuation via breakdown/breakout acceptance or micro pullbacks only; reduce counter-trend attempts and focus on confirmation (DT-SL-073).

rules.name: WHY-Based Analysis
rules.condition: You observe a significant move (breakout, breakdown, impulse candle, sudden reversal).
rules.interpretation: Price movement is driven by behavior: SL hit, exits/profit booking, fresh entry, or operator push.
rules.action: Before trading, answer 'why did this move happen?' and choose the most plausible driver using evidence (liquidity map, comfort, operator signatures, volatility).

rules.name: Exit-Based Movement (Profit Booking / Forced Exit)
rules.condition: After a strong move, price reverses without clear new-entry signals, often around holidays/events or after long continuation.
rules.interpretation: Moves can be driven by exits (profit booking or forced exit) rather than fresh entries.
rules.action: Do not assume reversal = new trend. Manage positions proactively (DT-SL-111) and demand confirmation before flipping bias (DT-DOW-003).

rules.name: Multi-Possibility Thinking
rules.condition: A setup appears around a key level/zone.
rules.interpretation: Multiple drivers can explain the same candle; single-story thinking causes bias.
rules.action: Generate 3-4 plausible drivers (SL hit, profit booking, fresh entry, operator push). Trade only after evidence supports one scenario and invalidates others.

rules.name: Breakdown Psychology (Forced Exit 'Himmat Tutna')
rules.condition: Participants hold losing positions for long, then a sudden acceleration occurs through a level with minimal bounce.
rules.interpretation: Forced exits can create sharp momentum and SL cascades.
rules.action: Avoid fading the acceleration. If aligned with structure/liquidity, trade with momentum after confirmation; if trapped, exit quickly.

rules.name: Momentum Source Classification
rules.condition: Any strong directional move occurs.
rules.interpretation: Not all momentum is SL-driven; classifying the source improves targeting and risk.
rules.action: Classify the move as one of: (1) SL hit/cascade, (2) profit booking/exits, (3) fresh entry breakout, (4) operator push/event-flow. Adjust tactics accordingly.

rules.name: Trap Before Big Move (Reinforced)
rules.condition: Price approaches a key level with rising comfort/participation and then shows hesitation or repeated failures.
rules.interpretation: Large moves often require liquidity creation; traps can occur before the real move.
rules.action: Avoid early entries into comfort. Wait for trap confirmation (failed break, sweep + reclaim) before committing.

rules.name: Expand Thinking Range
rules.condition: You can explain a move with only 1-2 obvious reasons (e.g., 'support' or 'resistance').
rules.interpretation: Shallow explanations increase mistake frequency in complex sessions.
rules.action: Expand to behavior-based reasons (who is trapped, who exited, what comfort formed, what liquidity was created) before acting.

rules.name: Trading Zone Classification (Discount / Fair / Inflated)
rules.condition: Before taking a trade, determine price context relative to recent value and extremes.
rules.interpretation: Edge is higher at extremes (discount/inflated) and lower at fair price.
rules.action: Classify the current location into: discount (cheap), fair (balanced), inflated (expensive). Use MATRIX-ZONE-001 posture rules.

rules.name: Fair Price Avoidance
rules.condition: Zone is classified as fair (balanced) and no strong confluence exists.
rules.interpretation: Fair price has low edge and high chop; fake moves increase.
rules.action: Avoid trading by default. Only trade if strong confluence exists (liquidity + confirmation + operator/comfort evidence).

rules.name: Zone-Based Entry Alignment
rules.condition: A setup is identified (reclaim, break, SL hunt, comfort).
rules.interpretation: Setups work best when aligned with extreme zones.
rules.action: Prioritize trades when zone is discount or inflated. Deprioritize or skip similar setups in fair zone.

rules.name: SL + Extreme Zone Confluence
rules.condition: A clear SL cluster exists and zone is discount or inflated.
rules.interpretation: Liquidity behavior becomes more exploitable at extremes; traps/continuations are cleaner.
rules.action: Increase setup priority when SL clusters align with discount/inflated zones, still requiring confirmation (DT-SL-073).

rules.name: Volume Profile for Fair-Price Detection (Optional)
rules.condition: Volume profile / VWAP / high-volume node data is available.
rules.interpretation: Fair price is often near value area / high-volume nodes where two-way trade dominates.
rules.action: Use volume profile/VWAP to approximate fair zone. If price is inside value, treat as fair and reduce trading frequency.

rules.name: Gap-to-Zone Behavior
rules.condition: Market opens with a gap that lands into discount/fair/inflated zone.
rules.interpretation: Gap location influences edge and trap probability.
rules.action: Gap into discount: prefer longs after confirmation. Gap into inflated: expect comfort/trap or continuation based on operator holding. Gap into fair: avoid by default.

rules.name: Zone-Based Risk Posture
rules.condition: Zone is classified (discount/fair/inflated).
rules.interpretation: Risk differs by zone due to edge and chop probability.
rules.action: Discount: allow slightly more aggressive posture. Inflated: trade cautiously and prefer confirmation. Fair: avoid or trade minimal size.

rules.name: Plan vs Reality (Abandon Plan on Mismatch)
rules.condition: You prepared a pre-market plan (gap up/down/flat scenarios) but the opening behavior contradicts it.
rules.interpretation: The plan is a hypothesis; reality overrides hypothesis.
rules.action: Abandon the plan immediately and switch to observation + rebuild mode (aligns with DT-SL-071/028).

rules.name: No Trade on Unclear Open (Wait 10-20 minutes)
rules.condition: Opening is unexpectedly volatile, contradicts hypothesis, or shows unstable whipsaw patterns.
rules.interpretation: Early uncertainty increases trap probability and forced errors.
rules.action: Wait 10-20 minutes, let structure form, then trade only after confirmation and zone alignment.

rules.name: Second Opportunity Engine
rules.condition: First setup fails or is missed, and the market builds a new structure later.
rules.interpretation: Professionals wait for the next clean opportunity rather than forcing the first idea.
rules.action: Reset, rebuild hypothesis, and trade the next confirmed setup (within attempt limits).

rules.name: Trader Removal (Side Exhaustion)
rules.condition: A strong directional move occurs with continued follow-through and minimal counter response.
rules.interpretation: One side may be removed/exhausted; next liquidity target can shift.
rules.action: After removal, expect retracement traps and target shifts (DT-SL-116/151). Do not assume immediate continuation forever (DT-SL-153).
