rules.name: Retracement Trap After Strong Move
rules.condition: After a strong impulse, a retracement invites late participants on the opposite side.
rules.interpretation: Late entries create fresh SL fuel for the next continuation/reversal.
rules.action: Treat the retracement as potential liquidity creation. Enter only after confirmation (failed reclaim, sweep+reclaim, or acceptance).

rules.name: Cross-Index Divergence
rules.condition: One index shows strength while another weakens (or lags) during the same window.
rules.interpretation: Divergence can signal operator rotation/trap; correlation may not confirm the trade.
rules.action: Reduce conviction and size; require stronger confirmation and zone alignment before trading the weaker/lagging index.

rules.name: One-Side Market Limitation
rules.condition: Only one side is active (only buyers or only sellers) and participation dries up on the opposite side.
rules.interpretation: Markets often need two-sided participation to continue; edge reduces when liquidity is absent.
rules.action: Expect stall, liquidity creation, or reversal. Reduce aggressive continuation trades and wait for new participation signals.

rules.name: Position Holding Behavior Creates Future Liquidity
rules.condition: Participants hold losing trades (or average) instead of exiting quickly.
rules.interpretation: Held positions become future forced-exit/SL pools.
rules.action: Include held-loss behavior in liquidity map (with profit holders) and anticipate future liquidation moves.

rules.name: Loss Analysis (Mistake -> Assumption)
rules.condition: A stopped-out trade occurs.
rules.interpretation: Loss is feedback; the error is often a wrong assumption about driver/zone/liquidity readiness.
rules.action: Record which assumption failed (WHY driver, comfort, zone, operator exit, validity) and update filters before re-entry.

rules.name: News Avoidance / Pre-Event Exit
rules.condition: Major scheduled news/events are near or event risk is elevated.
rules.interpretation: Event volatility can invalidate SL/zone logic and cause random spikes.
rules.action: Exit or reduce exposure before events; avoid holding positions through major releases unless explicitly designed for it.

rules.name: Chart Linking (Move -> Reaction -> Next Move)
rules.condition: A notable move occurs (break, impulse, sweep, rejection) and a new local structure forms after it.
rules.interpretation: Every move creates new expectations, new comfort, and new SL pools; analyzing candles in isolation misses the next trap.
rules.action: After each major move, predict trader reaction (buy dip/sell rally/fade breakout) and map where the next liquidity will form; trade only after confirmation.

rules.name: Continuation Illusion Trap (Overextended != Reversal)
rules.condition: Market has moved strongly and the crowd assumes 'it must reverse now'.
rules.interpretation: Overextended moves often continue because reversal-thinking creates the next trap/SL pool.
rules.action: Do not fade purely because it 'moved a lot'. Require reversal confirmation (operator exit + structure shift + acceptance) before counter-trend trades.

rules.name: News Behavior (Step Movement)
rules.condition: Negative/positive news is active or anticipated and price behavior becomes staged rather than clean trend.
rules.interpretation: News-driven markets can form supports/resistances step-by-step before continuing; direct extrapolation is unreliable.
rules.action: Reduce aggression, require stronger confirmation, and prioritize risk control (DT-SL-156/100).

rules.name: Support-Based Downtrend Progression
rules.condition: A downtrend is active and price repeatedly creates small supports before breaking them.
rules.interpretation: Supports are created to invite dip-buyers and late sellers; breaks create continuation fuel.
rules.action: In downtrend, treat repeated support formation as potential trap/continuation steps; sell breakdown acceptance or sell failed reclaim after breaks.

rules.name: Resistance-Based Uptrend Progression
rules.condition: An uptrend is active and price repeatedly creates small resistances before breaking them.
rules.interpretation: Resistances invite sellers; break + acceptance continues trend and can trigger SL cascades.
rules.action: In uptrend, trade break + acceptance or buy pullbacks after confirmation; avoid shorting resistances without exit/shift evidence.

rules.name: Trap Formation Requires Two-Sided Participation
rules.condition: You expect a trap/SL hunt but only one side is active and the other side is absent/scared.
rules.interpretation: Traps need both buyers and sellers to create liquidity; one-sided markets often stall or create liquidity first.
rules.action: Do not force a trap narrative. Wait for two-sided participation signals or treat as low-quality environment (aligns with DT-SL-153/096).

rules.name: Large Gap -> New Chart Priority
rules.condition: Gap is large relative to recent volatility and opens away from prior structure.
rules.interpretation: Old structure may lose relevance; a new intraday structure forms quickly after open.
rules.action: Reduce reliance on previous micro-levels; wait for new structure/acceptance in first 10-20 minutes before committing.

rules.name: Gap Behavior (Pause vs Recovery)
rules.condition: Market opens with a gap and the first sequence shows either direct continuation or strong recovery.
rules.interpretation: Gap-up often needs pause/sideways/pullback before sustainable continuation; gap-down with strong recovery candle can increase reversal probability.
rules.action: Avoid instant entries. Use opening matrix + microstructure confirmation to decide continuation vs reversal.

rules.name: Partial Capture Rule
rules.condition: A move is in progress and you have a confirmed entry.
rules.interpretation: Capturing the entire move is unrealistic; late holding increases giveback and trap risk.
rules.action: Capture the logical portion (first/clean leg) and manage exits using operator exit signals and structure; avoid aiming for perfect tops/bottoms.

rules.name: Overtrading Control (Max 2-3 Trades/Day)
rules.condition: You have already taken multiple trades in the session or clarity is degrading.
rules.interpretation: More trades often mean more confusion and lower edge; drawdowns come from overtrading.
rules.action: Cap at 2-3 trades/day unless the market is exceptionally clean and all filters align. Stop early on mismatch signals (DT-SL-049).

rules.name: Reaction Prediction (What Will Traders Do Next?)
rules.condition: After a move, you are evaluating whether to continue, reverse, or wait.
rules.interpretation: Trader reaction drives the next liquidity pool and trap; anticipating reaction improves timing.
rules.action: Ask: what will the crowd do next (buy dip, sell rally, chase breakout)? Map where their SL will sit and wait for confirmation before entry.

rules.name: Highest Probability Side Selection
rules.condition: Both long and short narratives exist (dual scenario) and you must choose a side.
rules.interpretation: The obvious direction is not always the highest probability; probability comes from SL availability + validity + zone + confirmation.
rules.action: Trade the side with higher SL availability and clearer liquidity path, after confirmation. If probability is unclear, do not trade.

rules.name: Reason-Based Trading (No Reason -> No Trade)
rules.condition: You are about to enter based on impulse, FOMO, or pattern without a clear driver.
rules.interpretation: Trades without a clear reason are usually comfort-chasing and become liquidity.
rules.action: Require a stated reason (WHY driver + SL map + zone + confirmation). If you cannot state it, skip.

rules.name: SL Availability Rule (No SL -> SL Creation -> Then Move)
rules.condition: You expect a strong move but there is no obvious SL liquidity on the target side.
rules.interpretation: Market often creates SL first by moving opposite to attract participants, then targets the created SL.
rules.action: Wait for SL creation behavior (comfort/late entries) before expecting the real move. Do not force direction early.

rules.name: SL Creation Phase (First Move Can Be Opposite)
rules.condition: Market is stalling and participation is one-sided or low, but a larger move is expected.
rules.interpretation: First move can be engineered to create new SL pools; the second move targets them.
rules.action: Treat the first engineered move as potential trap/liquidity creation. Enter only after confirmation of the real direction.

rules.name: Target the Weak Side (Losing Traders)
rules.condition: Selecting targets for SL hunting.
rules.interpretation: Losing/trapped traders provide forced liquidity; deeply profitable traders may exit manually (DT-SL-095).
rules.action: Prefer targeting losing/trapped participants and fresh SL pools; deprioritize targets based on assumed SL of large profit holders.

rules.name: No Chase Rule (Move Done -> Skip)
rules.condition: A big move already happened and you missed the entry.
rules.interpretation: Late entries become liquidity and get trapped.
rules.action: Skip chasing. Wait for retracement + confirmation or a new setup (DT-SL-079/149).

rules.name: Late Entry Trap (Big Candle Entry = Liquidity)
rules.condition: You enter after multiple large candles or at the end of an impulse without pullback/acceptance.
rules.interpretation: Late entries often become the next liquidity pool and get reversed on.
rules.action: Avoid entries after big candles unless acceptance + continuation is confirmed. Prefer pullback/scale-in with defined risk.

rules.name: Opposite Trade Logic (When SL Alignment Demands It)
rules.condition: Chart direction looks obvious but SL/comfort/operator evidence suggests the opposite side is higher probability.
rules.interpretation: Markets can move opposite to obvious direction to target comfort-based SL pools.
rules.action: Only take opposite trades after strong confirmation (failed break, sweep+reclaim, operator exit) and with reduced size.

rules.name: Overcrowding Filter (All Indices Same Direction)
rules.condition: Correlated indices are all moving strongly in the same direction with high comfort.
rules.interpretation: Continuation edge can reduce due to overcrowding; retracement/trap probability increases.
rules.action: Avoid late continuation entries. Prefer waiting for pullback, trap confirmation, or operator holding evidence before adding risk.

rules.name: Early Move Capture (Avoid Late Move)
rules.condition: A move is underway and you must decide whether to participate late.
rules.interpretation: The best edge is early/clean legs; late legs carry trap risk.
rules.action: Participate early after confirmation or skip and wait for next setup. Combine with DT-SL-165 partial capture.

rules.name: Multi-Day Thinking (2-3 Day Expectation)
rules.condition: Before session open and when building bias.
rules.interpretation: Operators plan multi-day moves; thinking beyond 'tomorrow up/down' improves scenario planning.
rules.action: Outline 2-3 day possibilities (direct trend, gap-trap-continue, sideways-then-break). Trade only when day-1 behavior confirms one scenario.

rules.name: Operator Planning (Multi-Day Movement)
rules.condition: Assessing trend continuation vs pause after a strong move.
rules.interpretation: Operators often distribute/accumulate across days; immediate repetition of the same pattern is less likely.
rules.action: After a strong trend day, expect pause/sideways/trap next; reduce trend-chasing and rely on confirmation.

rules.name: Non-Repetition Rule (Pattern Won't Repeat Immediately)
rules.condition: Market already executed a strong, obvious pattern recently (clean trend/cascade).
rules.interpretation: Immediate repetition is less likely; the market often shifts to trap/sideways to create new liquidity.
rules.action: Adjust expectations: look for consolidation/traps and require stronger confirmation for continuation.

rules.name: Scenario Building (Gap Up/Down/Flat)
rules.condition: Before session open.
rules.interpretation: Scenario planning prevents rigid bias and improves reaction speed.
rules.action: Always build 3 scenarios (gap up/down/flat) with invalidation rules and preferred actions; treat plan as hypothesis (DT-SL-147).

rules.name: Dual-Side Thinking (Check Both Sides)
rules.condition: Before entering around a key level/zone.
rules.interpretation: Both buy-case and sell-case can be valid; ignoring one side increases trap risk.
rules.action: Explicitly write both cases (buy and sell), then let live confirmation choose the side (DT-SL-073/168).

rules.name: Flexible Execution (Plan != Execution)
rules.condition: A plan is prepared but live market behavior diverges.
rules.interpretation: Profit comes from adapting execution to reality, not from defending a plan.
rules.action: Adapt immediately: wait, rebuild, or switch (DT-SL-071/147/148).

rules.name: Expectation Control (Prepare Possibilities, Not Certainty)
rules.condition: You feel certain about one outcome before confirmation.
rules.interpretation: Certainty increases bias and overtrading.
rules.action: Replace certainty with 3 possibilities and invalidation points; trade only after evidence selects one path.

rules.name: Immediate Exit on Failure (No Hope Holding)
rules.condition: Your trade thesis invalidates (breaks structure, confirmation fails, operator exit signs appear).
rules.interpretation: Hope holding creates large losses and destroys expectancy.
rules.action: Exit immediately when invalidated; never convert intraday trades into positional holds.

rules.name: Rule-Based Profit (No Random Trades)
rules.condition: Considering a trade outside the rulebook filters.
rules.interpretation: Random trades remove the edge and increase drawdown.
rules.action: If the trade does not pass zone + validity + SL + confirmation + probability selection, do not trade.

rules.name: Selective Opportunity Focus
rules.condition: Market is choppy or multiple low-quality setups appear.
rules.interpretation: Not trading is a position; selective trading preserves capital for high-quality moves.
rules.action: Wait for clear setups with confluence; stop trading early if clarity is lost.

rules.name: Fixed RR Module (Target 75 / SL 30) - Eligibility Gate
rules.condition: You want to execute a fixed R:R intraday setup with Target = 75 points and SL = 30 points.
rules.interpretation: Fixed targets only work when (1) the next logical liquidity/structure target is at least 75 points away and (2) the structural invalidation can be expressed within 30 points. Forcing 75/30 in the wrong regime increases stop-outs.
rules.action: Use 75/30 only if: (A) zone is discount or inflated (DT-SL-140/141), (B) SL-hunting validity filters pass (DT-SL-094..102), (C) entry is after confirmation (DT-SL-073/190) and the invalidation fits within 30 points WITHOUT widening later (DT-SL-036/041/053). If invalidation requires more than 30, skip or use a different module; do not compress SL into noise.

rules.name: Sweep + Reclaim Trap Reversal (Target 75 / SL 30)
rules.condition: Price sweeps a highly visible level (PDH/PDL/round number/prior swing) and then reclaims with strong acceptance (DT-SL-190), ideally at a zone extreme (DT-SL-140/143).
rules.interpretation: The sweep collects clustered stops at the obvious level (DT-SL-194/196). The reclaim + acceptance confirms the trap and increases probability of a move to the next liquidity pool.
rules.action: Enter only after strong reclaim/acceptance (DT-SL-190) and 1m reaction confirmation (DT-SL-122/123). Place SL = 30 points beyond the reclaim invalidation (beyond the micro-sweep extreme if it still fits inside 30; otherwise skip the 75/30 module). Set TP = 75 points toward the next mapped liquidity/structure target; do not hold if reclaim fails (DT-SL-185).

rules.name: Accepted Break + Pullback Continuation (Target 75 / SL 30)
rules.condition: A key level breaks with strong acceptance (DT-DOW-002 / DT-SL-190), followed by a controlled pullback/hold above (for longs) or below (for shorts). Avoid range-farming conditions (DT-SL-055).
rules.interpretation: Acceptance reduces fake-break probability. The pullback creates a clean pivot to express risk tightly without placing SL at the obvious cluster (DT-SL-196).
rules.action: Enter on pullback hold only after acceptance is proven (DT-SL-190). Place SL = 30 points beyond the pullback pivot/invalidation (not on the exact level; use buffer logic (DT-SL-196) but keep total SL = 30). Set TP = 75 points toward the next liquidity pool; if price returns and loses acceptance (reclaim failure), exit immediately (DT-SL-185).

rules.name: Break Failure (Failed Acceptance) Reversal (Target 75 / SL 30)
rules.condition: A breakout/breakdown occurs but fails acceptance (DT-DOW-002) and then reclaims back into the prior range with confirmation (DT-SL-190/122).
rules.interpretation: Failed acceptance often indicates a liquidity grab and a trap. The reclaim provides the confirmation gate and a clear invalidation point.
rules.action: Enter only after failed-acceptance is confirmed and a strong reclaim occurs (DT-SL-190). Place SL = 30 points beyond the failure extreme/pivot (if the logical invalidation is wider than 30, skip the 75/30 module). Set TP = 75 points toward the opposite-side liquidity pool (range edge/PD level).

rules.name: Volatility-Era Profit Booking (Mandatory for Regular Size)
rules.condition: Intraday volatility is high (DT-SL-209) or the session repeatedly shows 'profit -> sharp reversal -> stop-out' behavior; you are trading regular size (not tiny learning size).
rules.interpretation: In modern whipsaw regimes, waiting for full targets often converts visible profit into loss. Expectancy improves when you lock profit early and reduce exposure to reversal hunts.
rules.action: Pre-plan a first profit objective at the nearest logical liquidity/structure level (DT-SL-067/165). On first objective hit: book partial and/or tighten risk (move SL to safer invalidation; never widen). If price becomes choppy near the objective (operator exit signs), exit the remainder (DT-SL-093/185).

rules.name: Profit->Loss Psychology Trap (Do Not Stare at Profit)
rules.condition: You see a trade in profit and start expecting a 'big winner' without fresh liquidity/acceptance evidence; or you delay exit because the target is close.
rules.interpretation: Markets often show profit first to anchor greed, then reverse fast to force emotional exits. This is a psychological trap that breaks discipline more than analysis.
rules.action: Treat visible profit as a signal to execute your management rules (DT-SL-217/067). If the market fails to continue after a profit window (stall + rejection), take the profit and wait for the next setup (DT-SL-173/149).

rules.name: No Direct Buy into Active Selling (Require a Logical Support / Pivot)
rules.condition: Buyers are already in profit from a prior up-move and active selling is visible (supply/pressure).
rules.interpretation: Buying immediately during active selling is usually prediction. If reversal is real, it will show confirmation at a logical support/pivot (close/VWAP/value edge/swing).
rules.action: Do not buy just because price looks cheap. Wait for a logical support/pivot to show acceptance/reclaim (DT-SL-190/073) and then buy. If support does not hold, skip; do not average (DT-SL-041).

rules.name: Previous Close Pivot (Close = First Support/Resistance Candidate)
rules.condition: Planning and executing around the prior session close, especially after gap days or high-volatility sessions.
rules.interpretation: The close often represents equilibrium/fair value where many participants are anchored. Price frequently reacts there to trap late buyers/sellers or to confirm a real trend shift.
rules.action: Mark prior close as a pivot. In gap-down recoveries, reclaiming close with acceptance increases reversal probability. In gap-up failures, losing close increases downside SL-hunt probability. Use it as a confirmation gate; avoid trading the middle around it without clarity (DT-SL-141/208).

rules.name: Anti-Pattern-Overfit (Market Does Not Repeat Yesterday's Script)
rules.condition: You attempt to apply yesterday's exact pattern (e.g., 'yesterday close-resistance caused a drop, so today will do the same').
rules.interpretation: Markets rarely replay the same script immediately; psychology shifts after multi-day moves, traps, news, or positioning changes. Overfitting causes repeated stop-outs in the new regime.
rules.action: Use yesterday only to map liquidity and anchors (PDH/PDL/close). Build 3 scenarios (gap up/down/flat) and wait for today's confirmation/acceptance before committing (DT-SL-180/181/147).

rules.name: Swing High/Low = SL Pool (Intraday Liquidity)
rules.condition: A swing high or swing low is confirmed on 1m/5m Dow structure.
rules.interpretation: Confirmed swings attract stops: above swing highs sit seller SLs (short stops); below swing lows sit buyer SLs (long stops). These levels are frequent sweep targets and trap pivots, especially when they align with PDH/PDL/round numbers/value edges.
rules.action: Always add the last confirmed swing high/low (1m and 5m) to the liquidity map. Prefer trades that target these pools or use sweep+reclaim around them (DT-SL-190/210).

rules.name: Swing Reclaim Trigger (Trap Confirmation)
rules.condition: Price sweeps beyond a confirmed swing high/low and then reclaims back across it with strong acceptance (DT-SL-190).
rules.interpretation: The sweep takes clustered stops at the swing. The reclaim shows failure to accept the break; trapped late traders become fuel for the reversal/continuation leg.
rules.action: Enter only after reclaim + acceptance is visible (do not buy/sell on the touch). Place SL beyond the sweep extreme (with buffer) rather than on the swing level (DT-SL-194/196).

rules.name: 1m Execution, 5m Swing Confirmation Gate
rules.condition: A 1m setup appears around a swing but 5m structure is not aligned/confirmed.
rules.interpretation: Many 1m swings are noise; 5m swings represent higher-quality liquidity pools and reduce whipsaw entries.
rules.action: Use 1m for entry precision, but require 5m confirmation for higher confidence: prefer trades where 1m reclaim aligns with 5m swing context or where 5m structure is not contradicting the entry (DT-DOW-003). If 5m is unclear, reduce size or WAIT (DT-SL-148/209).

rules.name: Swing-Stop Placement (Avoid Exact Swing Levels)
rules.condition: Placing SL near a recent swing high/low or round number.
rules.interpretation: Stops cluster exactly at swings; micro-sweeps commonly pierce the swing by a few points before moving in the intended direction.
rules.action: Do not place SL exactly on the swing. Place SL beyond the micro-sweep buffer (DT-SL-196). If that makes SL too wide, skip the trade or reduce size; never widen after entry (DT-SL-041/053).

integration_conflicts.note: Opening candle is a pre-filter and context engine; reclaim/structure-based entries remain primary. If opening candle signals 'trap/volatile', prioritize avoiding trades even if micro-reclaims appear.
PDH/PDL/round numbers remain important, but how they are treated (support vs resistance) must align with directional bias.
Premarket analysis produces a hypothesis, not a commitment. Live confirmation can veto the hypothesis and trigger DT-SL-071 dynamic plan switching.
On event-driven or flow-dominant sessions (DT-SL-099/100), SL-hunting targets can be unreliable; trade smaller or avoid and prioritize confirmation.
Comfort signals increase trap probability but do not guarantee reversal. Require confirmation (failed break, sweep+reclaim, operator exit) before contrarian entries (DT-SL-103..110).
SL hunting uses reaction/reclaim behavior on 1m (DT-SL-122/123). Higher-TF closes remain useful for structure hypothesis (DT-DOW-*), but do not override micro reaction in execution.
Hold-with-operator (DT-SL-088) aims to capture continuation, but DT-SL-111 allows partial profit-taking when exit/distribution signatures appear (DT-SL-086/093).
Zone engine (DT-SL-140..146) can veto otherwise-valid setups when price is in fair zone (DT-SL-141). Treat fair zone as a default no-trade condition unless exceptional confluence exists.
Waiting 10-20 minutes on unclear open (DT-SL-148) can miss early moves. This is intentional to reduce trap losses; use DT-SL-132 to trade no-pullback trend days only after confirmation.

WHY-based classification (DT-SL-133..137) improves decisions but can become storytelling. Always anchor 'why' to observable evidence (liquidity, operator exit/holding, comfort, volatility) and require price confirmation (DT-SL-073).
Flow linking (DT-SL-157/167) improves anticipation but can become prediction. Use flow to map likely liquidity creation, not to front-run entries; confirmation still gates execution (DT-SL-073).

integration_conflicts.topic: Large Gap New Chart vs Previous Levels
integration_conflicts.note: On large gaps (DT-SL-163), previous micro levels may lose relevance. Keep PDH/PDL as macro anchors but rebuild intraday structure before trading aggressively.
DT-SL-166 caps trades/day to reduce drawdown. If you exceed the cap, it must be because all filters align and the market is unusually clean; otherwise stop trading.

integration_conflicts.topic: Tight SL vs Adaptive SL Range
integration_conflicts.note: DT-SL-036 prefers tight SL due to path uncertainty. DT-SL-053 allows a slightly wider SL only if it is pre-defined before entry and position size is reduced. Never widen SL after entry.
DT-SL-067 prioritizes first profit capture near ~1R to reduce giveback and emotion. Holding-with-operator (DT-SL-088) is allowed only as a small runner when continuation/holding evidence persists.
